reviews.review_msg: James can ramble on and on and go on tangents but his exams are generally fair if you study.
Lots of memorization needed, but James answers all your questions in lab and is happy to provide help in office hours as well!
James is very helpful, and his slides are top notch! Very approachable during class you can ask him whatever questions you have and he will help a lot!
James puts far too much weight on exams there is no margin for error. If you don't mind working super hard for a C- take his class, otherwise avoid James like he's lava
absolutely no remorse when grading your paper

James is a wealth of knowledge, and I really respect him, however he expects a LOT from his students and you can't slack off at all in this class. Put in the work, ask lots of questions, and do your best to understand the material. Luckily, he gives half his exam questions ahead of time. Also, don't rush through labs. Do them properly.

EASC310W "Paleontology" is NOT about dinosaurs. It's about microfossils and worms. The tests are very comprehensive, James is a very harsh grader and provides detailed feedback on writing assignments. The class would fail if it weren't for the TAs grading lab and lecture exams. He is a tough grader and expects you to know everything.

Very knowledgeable professor and great at teaching, the only tough part about this course is the amount of work given to you, expect to study more than usual. His slides and presentations were extremely clear. Very easy to talk to and to ask questions, highly recommend him if you plan on taking easc201.

James is a great professor with clear expectations. He puts a great deal of detail into lectures so take keen notes and review them post lecture. The tests are hard and compare/contrast details are important. I had online lectures which were pretty dry, but when James spoke in person at the lab he was not dry at all.

James is extremely sharp and knowledgeable about Earth Sciences. However, he is quite a dry lecturer and goes into GREAT detail about everything. Staying awake in lectures is difficult, but worth it. Tests are all VERY difficult. Brute memorization is key, however don't expect to do well on the exams. Try to make up your mark on the labs.
